backend.name: PHP
backend.opinion: My foundation — 9 years of building with it, from WordPress to Symfony to custom frameworks.

backend.name: Symfony
backend.opinion: My go-to for complex backend systems. Clean architecture, powerful DI, battle-tested.

backend.name: Node.js
backend.opinion: Great for API layers and tooling. I use it alongside PHP, not as a replacement.

backend.name: Laravel
backend.opinion: Used for client projects at TechRostrum. Prefer Symfony for complex systems.

backend.name: REST APIs
backend.opinion: Designed and consumed hundreds of API integrations across platforms.

backend.name: WordPress
backend.opinion: Custom plugin development, CMS migrations, WooCommerce integrations.

backend.name: Drupal
backend.opinion: Used at OBG and Bayview. Migrated Drupal systems to WordPress.

backend.name: Slim
backend.opinion: Lightweight and fast for API-driven products. Built a SaaS lead generation platform and meal planner with it.

frontend.name: React
frontend.opinion: My primary frontend framework. Used across SaaS platforms and client projects.

frontend.name: Next.js
frontend.opinion: Used for the AI SaaS platform and this portfolio. SSR + API routes make it versatile.

frontend.name: Tailwind CSS
frontend.opinion: Fast, consistent, and pairs perfectly with component-based architecture.

frontend.name: Bootstrap
frontend.opinion: Reliable for rapid prototyping and legacy projects.

frontend.name: Backbone/Marionette
frontend.opinion: Legacy frontend at StoreKeeper. Maintained and extended it.

frontend.name: Angular
frontend.opinion: Used for side projects including a custom authoring system. Understand the framework's patterns and DI model.

mobile.name: React Native
mobile.opinion: Built mobile experiences. Understand cross-platform tradeoffs.

mobile.name: Java
mobile.opinion: Built native Android apps during early freelance work. Foundation for understanding mobile architecture.

infrastructure.name: Kubernetes
infrastructure.opinion: Used at StoreKeeper for container orchestration. Understand deployment patterns.

infrastructure.name: Docker
infrastructure.opinion: Standard part of my workflow since 2019. Introduced Docker-based dev environments at Bayview.

infrastructure.name: Vercel
infrastructure.opinion: Used for Next.js deployments. Zero-config, reliable.

infrastructure.name: Scalingo
infrastructure.opinion: PaaS hosting for Symfony apps. Used at StoreKeeper since CC.Talent and for the AI SaaS.

infrastructure.name: Jenkins
infrastructure.opinion: Used alongside GitLab CI for deployment automation.

infrastructure.name: Linux Servers
infrastructure.opinion: Server migration, optimization, and maintenance at OBG.

infrastructure.name: GCP
infrastructure.opinion: Cloud hosting and services during agency work at TechRostrum and freelance projects.

infrastructure.name: TeamCity
infrastructure.opinion: CI/CD at StoreKeeper. Configured build pipelines and deployment automation.

infrastructure.name: Cypress
infrastructure.opinion: Implemented frontend automated testing at StoreKeeper, collaborating with QA to build reliable test coverage.

infrastructure.name: Sentry
infrastructure.opinion: Error tracking and performance monitoring at StoreKeeper. Essential for production visibility.

database.name: PostgreSQL
database.opinion: My default for anything transactional. Used at StoreKeeper for the core SaaS platform.

database.name: MySQL
database.opinion: Used across nearly every project. Solid, reliable, well-understood.

database.name: MariaDB
database.opinion: Used for PartsUnited systems at CC.Talent.

database.name: Redis
database.opinion: Caching and session management at StoreKeeper.

integration.name: Shopify
integration.opinion: Order sync, product management, inventory management via API.

integration.name: Magento 2
integration.opinion: Complex API integration for multi-platform sync.

integration.name: Shopware 6
integration.opinion: Platform integration for European e-commerce clients.

integration.name: CCV
integration.opinion: Commerce platform integration for Dutch market.
